message:
test(lifecycle): health status/healthy/initialized internal consistency (ibuuh.23)

Internal-consistency matrix row. cass health --json emits three top-level
contract fields that agents branch on — status (string), healthy (bool),
initialized (bool). The robot-mode contract requires they agree:
 * initialized=false => status='not_initialized' AND healthy=false
 * status in healthy-family ('healthy' | 'ok') <=> healthy=true
 * healthy=false requires non-empty errors OR non-healthy status (so the
   operator always sees WHY)

Silent drift (e.g. status='healthy' but healthy=false, or initialized=
false without the matching status) silently breaks any agent that picks
one field over another for its gating logic.

New test health_status_and_healthy_flag_are_internally_consistent
encodes the three invariants against the existing empty-HOME shape
already stable per my row 3.

VERIFICATION:
cargo test --test lifecycle_matrix: 61/61 pass.

Follow-up slice on coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.23; bead stays open
for the scheduler-backed rows still blocked on upstream ibuuh.30/.32.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
